Automotive AI Infrastructure

DriveLink: The Decentralized Backbone for Automotive AI

"Connecting OEMs, Data, and Intelligence via a secure, blockchain-powered standard."

Scroll to Explore

The Decentralized Backbone

Solving the core challenges of automotive AI through a unified, secure protocol.

The Core Problem

High costs of high-definition maps, lack of data standardization, and siloed automotive ecosystems.

The Solution

Unified data protocol, decentralized AI training, and incentive-based data sharing.

Our Technology

Leveraging decentralized infrastructure to create the ultimate automotive AI backbone.

Why DriveLink Matters

Reduces blind-spot accidents
Makes merging & lane changes smoother
Eliminates brake-wave traffic
Enables cooperative autonomous driving
Enhances fleet efficiency
Creates a universal communication standard

Target Segments

EV Fleets
OEM Vehicle Manufacturers
Smart Cities
Autonomous Driving R&D Labs

Revenue Model

  • $
    Fleet SaaS subscription per vehicle
  • $
    OEM licensing + update fees
  • $
    Smart city corridor contracts
  • $
    High-margin analytics (intent heatmaps, conflict zones)

Our Origin Story

"Imagine a world where every vehicle learns from every other vehicle—DriveLink is the protocol making that real."

10 November

Ideation

The founders realized the modern driving world suffers because vehicles operate as isolated agents. No shared intent → late reactions → preventable accidents. DriveLink was born to fix this.
Validation

First Validation

The early concept was pitched to PedalStart mentors:

  • Harsirjan Kour (Core Team @ PedalStart)
  • Sayanee Bhowmik (Ex-VC, Startup Mentor)

They validated the idea, refined the vision, and guided the foundational direction.

Insight

Industry Insight

The team spoke with the CEO of Simple Energy, gaining crucial EV-industry insights into fleet movement patterns and real-world communication challenges.
26 November

First Investor Deck

The founders built a high-impact pitch deck and met a VC contact:

Debasis Chakraborty

CEO & Founder, Dariaan Consulting

December

SMVIT Competition

Participated in the SMVIT event. While we didn't take home a trophy, the technical advice we received was invaluable, helping us refine our system architecture and identify critical improvements.
December 2025

Dariaan Labs Visit

A turning point at Dariaan Labs. Deep technical feedback exposed our initial session gaps and wrong assumptions. This experience forced us to rethink our approach and fix fundamental issues in our protocol.
December 2025

NMIT Collaboration

Partnered with a technical team from NMIT for hardware support. Their expertise was crucial in moving our first autonomous system from theoretical models to a functional, working implementation.

Current Pipeline

Autonomous Software CompletedHardware Design FinalizedNext: Build & Integrate Hardware

Meet the Visionaries

Hruday

CEO & Chief Systems Architect

Leads vision, protocol architecture, high-level systems strategy, partnerships, and standardization roadmap.

Nikhil

CTO

Computer Science & Engineering

Builds the prediction engine, simulation environment, and V2V messaging intelligence.

Krishna

CPO

Mechanical Engineering

Handles hardware feasibility, integration, mechanical systems, and real-vehicle interfacing.
